working_tree_note: Modifications to App.tsx (WorkflowPlanCard hasRichPlan logic), backend/main.py (status normalizer pipeline_executed fix), tests/workflows/test_e2e_multi_step_workflows.py (intent-mock for HELIX_MOCK_MODE), tests/integration/test_execute_five_demo_golden_paths.py (allow pipeline_executed status), tests/unit/backend/test_phase25_benchmark_turn_fixtures.py (regression coverage for pipeline_executed), tests/demo_scenarios/framework/tracer.py (PEP 604 future annotations for Python 3.9 compat), frontend/e2e/visual.spec.ts + regenerated snapshots (CapabilityGrid replaced legacy placeholder), tests/evals/conftest.py (NEW: live-LLM mode + skip-when-no-credentials policy), artifacts/worklog.md. None pushed to remote yet.

unit_tests.note: 1 skipped = R runtime not installed (single_cell R-based dep). True failures = 0.

integration_tests.note: Default pytest mark filter excludes integration/e2e cases that need a live deployed backend; these 9 are the offline-runnable subset (golden-path TestClient flows).

evals.command: pytest tests/evals/ -q  (with HELIX_MOCK_MODE=0 via tests/evals/conftest.py, live OpenAI gpt-5.5)
evals.note: Threshold met (>= 0.90). 6 failures are routing-quality regressions clustered in plasmid/directed-evolution and quality_assessment routing — see worklog 2026-05-04 for triage list.

frontend_playwright.note: Visual snapshots regenerated for the May 2 → May 4 demo-scenarios growth (513 LOC added to demoScenarios.ts) and the CapabilityGrid empty-state replacement. Smoke tests verify the Server: healthy badge appears, command submission works, and file upload UI accepts CSV.

latency.note: p50 and p95 both well under thresholds. p50 +1.5s vs May 2 (14.3 → 15.8) is noise + LLM contention from the eval suite running in parallel. p99 outlier is a single 277s case (no p99 threshold defined). p95 is essentially flat (19.6 → 19.2).

require_fresh_frontend_build.note: Tested locally via Vite dev server (Playwright). Production EC2 frontend bundle MUST be rebuilt with VITE_API_BASE_URL= (empty / same-origin) before deploy — see blocker beta-bundle-mixed-content.

blockers.description: Currently-deployed frontend bundle at https://helix-beta.noricum-biosoft.com/ (index-DnhrdT75.js) was built with VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://helix--Publi-...elb.amazonaws.com (plain-HTTP internal AWS ALB). Browsers block all API calls as mixed content; SPA shows 'Server: error' and is fully non-functional. Backend itself is healthy (curl https://helix-beta.noricum-biosoft.com/health returns {status: healthy, ...}).
blockers.resolution: On EC2 host: cd <HELIX_ROOT> && HELIX_SKIP_PIP=1 ./scripts/ec2/update-from-git.sh   (rebuilds SPA with VITE_API_BASE_URL= empty, reloads systemd). Verify: curl -s https://helix-beta.noricum-biosoft.com/ | grep -o 'index-[^"]*\.js' returns a NEW hash, and the resulting bundle does NOT contain 'helix--Publi-'.

blockers.description: Working tree has 8 uncommitted file modifications (4 production fixes + 4 test/snapshot updates) plus 6 untracked files (4 fixtures, 2 probe scripts). The gate above was run against this dirty tree, so the readiness measurement reflects the post-fix code, not the current HEAD (7a41660). These changes must be committed before declaring HEAD release-ready.
blockers.resolution: Commit the production fixes (backend/main.py, frontend/src/App.tsx) and test/snapshot updates as one or two coherent commits, then re-run the gate against the clean tree to confirm the result reproduces.

blockers.description: 1 unit test (single_cell R-based) skipped because Rscript runtime not installed. Carries over from May 2 baseline.
blockers.resolution: Install R runtime in CI/CD environment (no impact on Python-only release path).

blockers.description: 6 of 79 router evals regressed on the live LLM run today. Plasmid/directed-evolution prompts misroute to mutate_sequence (3 cases); quality_assessment is shadowed by fastqc_quality_analysis (2 cases); plain 'cluster' word routes to clustering_analysis instead of bulk_rnaseq_analysis (1 case). Eval pass rate (92.4%) still meets the 90% threshold so this does not block release, but the regressions stem from the recent router rewrite (commit c1e1e18) and should be addressed in a follow-up before promoting beta to GA.
blockers.resolution: Triage in worklog 2026-05-04 § 'Router-quality regressions'. Likely fix is tightening the LLM router's tool-disambiguation prompt for plasmid vs mutate_sequence and adding a deterministic pre-router rule for plain 'cluster' (no FASTA) → no-route.

readiness_note: All threshold checks pass and all gate suites are green. Readiness is FALSE because (a) the live beta deploy is currently broken by a stale frontend bundle (high-severity blocker beta-bundle-mixed-content — fix is one command on the EC2 host), and (b) the gate was measured against an uncommitted working tree, not against HEAD. Once the bundle is rebuilt and the working tree is committed, the system clears all four release_requirements and is release-ready.
